headline: EPF governance reform stalled — 9 board seats still politically appointed
context: Despite the 2023 EPF governance reform proposal, nine of thirteen board positions remain politically appointed, with no independent director requirement implemented.

cards.big: 9 of 13 EPF board seats politically appointed — overseeing RM1.1 trillion in savings
cards.sub: Workers' retirement fund governed by political appointees, not independent professionals

cards.big: EPF Act 1991 Section 4 gives Finance Minister full appointment authority over board
cards.sub: No requirement for investment expertise, contributor representation, or independence

cards.big: EPF manages RM1.1 trillion — retirement savings of 15 million Malaysian workers
cards.sub: Political governance of the nation's largest pool of private savings creates systemic risk

cards.big: Australia's superannuation boards require 50% independent directors by law
cards.sub: Comparable retirement systems mandate professional governance over political loyalty

cards.big: When politicians control workers' retirement savings, the temptation to use them politically is inevitable
Should EPF contributors elect at least half of the board members managing their savings?
